chapter_title.ar: باب سِيرَةِ الْإِمَامِ فِي نَفْسِهِ وَ فِي المَطْعَمِ وَ الْمَلْبَسِ إِذَا وَلِيَ الْأَمْرَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 106 – The ways of the Imam<sup>asws</sup> regarding himself<sup>asws</sup> and regarding the meals and the clothing when he<sup>asws</sup> is the Master<sup>asws</sup> of the Command

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مختلف فيه - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/362)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مختلف فيه - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/362)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَوْماً جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ ذَكَرْتُ آلَ فُلَانٍ وَ مَا هُمْ فِيهِ مِنَ النَّعِيمِ فَقُلْتُ لَوْ كَانَ هَذَا إِلَيْكُمْ لَعِشْنَا مَعَكُمْ فَقَالَ هَيْهَاتَ يَا مُعَلَّى أَمَا وَ اللَّهِ أَنْ لَوْ كَانَ ذَاكَ مَا كَانَ إِلَّا سِيَاسَةَ اللَّيْلِ وَ سِيَاحَةَ النَّهَارِ وَ لُبْسَ الْخَشِنِ وَ أَكْلَ الْجَشِبِ فَزُوِيَ ذَلِكَ عَنَّا فَهَلْ رَأَيْتَ ظُلَامَةً قَطُّ صَيَّرَهَا اللَّهُ تَعَالَى نِعْمَةً إِلَّا هَذِهِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad Bin Usman, from Al Moalla Bin Khunays who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> one day, ‘May I be sacrificed to you<sup>asws</sup>! I remembered the family of so and so and what they are in from the Bounties, so I said, ‘If only that was for you<sup>asws</sup>, we would have lived with you<sup>asws</sup> (prosperously)’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Far be it, O Moalla! But, by Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, had it been that, it would not have been except for hard labour (of protection) at night and strenuous work by the day, and wearing the coarse, and eating the tasteless. So that was Impeded from us<sup>asws</sup>. Have you ever seen a looted property Allah, the Most High, has turned into a blessing except this one (easy life for you while unjust Abbassids are in rule)?’
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hammad ibn ‘Uthman from Mu‘alla ibn Khunays who has said the following. “One day I said to abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.), may Allah take my soul in service for your cause, I thought of the descendants of so and so (‘Abbasside) and the wealth they have at their disposal. Then I wished that had such wealth been at your disposal we would have lived with you prosperously.” The Imam (a.s.) said, “Never O Mu‘alla, by Allah, had it been as you wished, there would have been nothing but hard labor of protections at nights and strenuous work during the days, coarse garments and tasteless food. Thus, it is kept away from us. Have you ever seen a usurpation that Allah, the Most High, would cause to become a blessing except this?”
